examples: I've got a job for you: could you wash the dishes?
I didn't speak to many coworkers for the ﬁrst year on the job.

definitions.text: An economic role for which a person is paid.
examples: That surgeon has a great job.
He's been out of a job since being made redundant in January.

definitions.text: To do odd jobs or occasional work for hire.
To take the loss, usually in a demeaning or submissive manner.
To peck (of a bird); to poke or prod . .mw-parser-output .defdate{font-size:smaller}
To pierce or poke (someone or something), typically with a sharp or pointed object; to stab.
To hit (someone) with a quick, sharp punch; to jab.
A sudden thrust or stab; a jab or punch.
